opp.name: DelMonte_Mexico_S2C
opp.next_step: July 2 Rick: Champion (Marcela in Mexico) is connecting internally with Procurement Selection Committee. Timing of next steps will be provided. Meanwhile we will invite to Horizon

sources: Salesforce Q2/Q3 queries returned no pain/metrics fields populated
metrics.narrative: No business problem, quantified impact, or qualitative pain has been documented in any Salesforce field (Customer_Business_Problem__c, Business_Objectives__c, Value_to_Customer__c, Compelling_Event__c not present in org schema or returned empty). No Avoma calls exist to provide buyer-stated pain. The deal is one day old and in early Qualified stage, so absence of quantified metrics at this point is normal for discovery phase. The next move is to conduct a discovery call with Marcela and the procurement committee to elicit the operational pain, cost/cycle-time baseline, and desired business outcomes.

sources: Next Step 2026-07-02: 'Champion (Marcela in Mexico) is connecting internally with Procurement Selection Committee'
champion.narrative: Champion identified: Marcela (Mexico). The Next Step (2026-07-02) states that Marcela is the champion and is actively connecting internally with the Procurement Selection Committee. This shows she has internal access and is facilitating the evaluation. However, her title, her relationship to the Economic Buyer or Decision Maker, her authority within procurement, and her advocacy strength are unknown. No contact role exists for Marcela yet. No Avoma calls capture her engagement or quotes. The evidence shows she is the initial connector, but whether she can drive the deal, influence the committee, or has budget proximity is unconfirmed. The next move is to qualify Marcela's role, scope of influence, and relationship to the committee during the discovery call, and to assess whether she can advocate for Zycus inside the selection process.

sources: Salesforce Q3 competitive fields: not present or empty
competition.narrative: No competitors have been identified. Salesforce Competitors__c field returned empty. Salesforce fields for competitive context (How_are_you_addressing_your_problem_toda__c, Existing_vendor__c, Replacing_What__c, Why_not_Zycus__c) are not present or returned empty. No Avoma calls exist to capture competitive mentions. The deal is one day old, so it is plausible that the buyer has not yet disclosed their current stack or shortlist. The next move is to ask during discovery what they use today for contract management, savings tracking, and supplier collaboration, whether they are replacing an incumbent, and who else they are evaluating.

sources: Salesforce Q2/Q3 queries returned no pain fields
Product scope inferred: iContract, iSave, iSupplier, Merlin, Agentic AI, ANA
identify_pain.narrative: No specific operational or financial pain has been articulated by the buyer. Salesforce fields for pain (Customer_Business_Problem__c, Pain_points_in_the_current_solution__c, What_if_this_is_not_done__c) are not present or returned empty. No Avoma calls exist to capture buyer quotes. The product scope (full S2C suite: iContract, iSave, iSupplier, plus Merlin/Agentic AI/ANA) implies procurement inefficiency pain (manual contracting, limited supplier collaboration, no AI-driven negotiation), but this is inferred from the solution set, not stated by the buyer. The next move is to conduct a discovery call with Marcela and the procurement committee to elicit what is broken today, who owns the pain, and what the cost of inaction is.

sources: Salesforce Q2/Q3 queries returned no paper-process fields
paper_process.narrative: No contracting, legal, InfoSec, or partner involvement has been documented. Salesforce returned no values for Current_Contract_Expiration__c or any MSA/SOW-related fields. No Avoma calls exist. The deal is one day old and in early Qualified stage, so paper process details are not expected yet. The typical Del Monte enterprise motion will involve MSA/SOW negotiation, InfoSec review, and potentially ERP integration scoping (given iSupplier in scope), but none of this is visible. The next move is to ask during discovery whether an existing vendor contract is expiring (a forcing function) and what their procurement contracting timeline looks like.

sources: Next Step 2026-07-02 mentions procurement committee but no EB named
economic_buyer.narrative: No Economic Buyer has been identified. No contact roles exist on this opportunity yet. The Next Step (2026-07-02) references a 'Procurement Selection Committee' but does not name the budget authority or commercial decision-maker. In a Del Monte procurement motion, the EB is typically a senior procurement director or CFO-level sponsor. The evidence shows only that champion Marcela is the internal connector to the committee; her role and authority over budget are unknown. The next move is to map the committee members and identify who controls commercial terms and final sign-off.

sources: Next Step 2026-07-02: Marcela connecting with Procurement Selection Committee
decision_process.narrative: The Next Step (2026-07-02) indicates that champion Marcela is connecting internally with a 'Procurement Selection Committee', signaling a committee-driven vendor selection process, which is typical for enterprise S2C deals. However, the composition of the committee, approval chain, and timeline are unknown. No Salesforce fields (What_is_the_decision_process__c, Mandate__c, Purchase_approvals_Required_from__c) returned values. No contact roles or Avoma calls provide further detail. The evidence suggests a structured procurement process is beginning, but the formal steps (RFI, RFP, shortlist, approvals) and who participates at each gate are not yet mapped. The next move is to ask Marcela or the committee for the formal evaluation timeline and approval chain.

sources: Salesforce Q2/Q3 queries returned no decision-criteria fields
decision_criteria.narrative: No decision criteria have been documented. Salesforce fields Business_Requirements__c, Top_Challenges_Priorities__c, and AI_Needs_in_RFP_Rating__c are not present in the org schema or returned empty. No Avoma calls exist to capture evaluation criteria. The product scope (iContract, iSave, iSupplier, Merlin, Agentic AI, ANA) suggests a full S2C evaluation including AI/Merlin capability, but the buyer's prioritized requirements and scoring rubric are unknown. At one day old and Qualified stage, formal criteria are typically not yet established. The next move is to conduct a discovery session to understand their current procurement pain points, existing stack, and what they will measure vendors against.

cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. We're behind on this one — the reads below say why and what would change it.

blocks.read: We're well behind — this one is cold.
blocks.title: Zycus win position

bullets.full: Losing on capability fit — AIS fields (AIS_Score__c, AIS_Status__c, AIS_Why__c) are not populated. No Avoma calls exist to assess buyer AI appetite. Product scope includes Merlin, Agentic AI, and ANA, indicating Zycus is offering AI-driven negotiation and analytics, but whether the buyer is AI Hungry, AI Curious, or AI Resistant is unknown. The next move is to probe AI appetite and use-case fit during the discovery call.
bullets.text: Losing on capability fit — AIS fields (AIS_Score__c, AIS_Status__c, AIS_Why__c) are not populated.

The next move is to map the committee members and identify who controls commercial terms and final sign-off.

blocks.title: What moves it forward

cro_panel.header: Del Monte Foods, Inc — Qualified · $250K · closes 30 Jul

contributions.evidence: Rep driving cadence; customer not initiating.

commentary.deal_risk: Risk 14 is driven by Rep driving cadence; customer not initiating. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.win_position: Win 6 is carried by champion strength +0.30 (weight 15). Offsetting it: differentiation strength -0.50 (weight 20).
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 48 (roughly flat): Rep driving cadence; customer not initiating. Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Partial Read: 4 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, stage integrity, commercial, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment 49 reflects real customer investment: Security/procurement review underway; Customer owns 1 open commitment(s).
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 0 is anchored on the stage win (6), nudged by commitment 49 and momentum 48, and dragged by risk 14. Read is Partial Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.

contributions.evidence: Rep driving cadence; customer not initiating.
Next Step carries 1 dated milestone(s) — actively worked.

champion_strength.source: Next Step 2026-07-02: 'Champion (Marcela in Mexico) is connecting internally with Procurement Selection Committee'
champion_strength.summary: Champion Marcela (Mexico) identified in Next Step dated 2026-07-02. She is actively connecting internally with the Procurement Selection Committee, demonstrating initial engagement and internal access. However, her title, scope of influence, relationship to Economic Buyer/Decision Maker, and advocacy strength are unknown. No contact role exists for her yet. No Avoma calls capture her engagement. Evidence shows she is the initial connector but whether she can drive the deal or influence the committee is unconfirmed. Strength rated 'developing' because engagement is active but depth and influence are unproven.

items.commitment: Invite Marcela and the procurement committee to Horizon (customer/prospect event)

items.action: Secure a discovery call with champion Marcela and at least two members of the procurement committee to multi-thread, map Economic Buyer and Decision Maker, elicit quantified business problem (cost baseline, cycle time, error rate, savings target), understand current stack and whether replacing an incumbent, and confirm evaluation timeline and approval chain. Use the call to position Merlin/Agentic AI capability against their negotiation pain.
items.trigger: Champion Marcela connecting internally with committee; timing of next steps to be provided
items.expected_effect: Multi-thread the engagement, map EB/DM, begin MEDDPICC qualification, confirm buyer urgency and process, and position AI differentiation. Moves deal from single-threaded Qualified to multi-threaded discovery with clear next milestone.

items.action: Send Horizon event invite to Marcela and request she forward to the procurement committee members (or provide their names/emails so Zycus can invite directly). Use Horizon as a multi-threading and executive-access catalyst.
items.trigger: Next Step commitment to invite to Horizon
items.expected_effect: Fulfill the Horizon invite commitment, create opportunity to engage multiple committee members and potential executive sponsors in a lower-pressure setting, and demonstrate Zycus customer base and AI innovation.

items.action: Map the procurement committee composition and roles. During the discovery call or via Marcela, identify who sits on the committee, their titles, their functional areas (procurement, finance, legal, IT), and who among them controls budget and final vendor selection. Create contact roles in Salesforce for each.
items.trigger: Procurement committee mentioned but composition unknown
items.expected_effect: Break single-threading, identify Economic Buyer and Decision Maker, and enable targeted engagement strategy per stakeholder. Reduces risk of committee decision happening outside Zycus visibility.

items.action: Qualify Marcela's champion strength: her title, her role in procurement (buyer, analyst, manager, director), her relationship to the Economic Buyer and Decision Maker, her scope of influence over the committee, and evidence of her advocacy for Zycus (e.g., has she shared internal priorities, pushed back on competitors for us, or volunteered to drive the evaluation). Document in contact role and Next Step.
items.trigger: Marcela identified as champion but title, authority, and advocacy strength unknown
items.expected_effect: Confirm whether Marcela can drive the deal or whether a stronger champion/coach is needed. If she lacks authority or EB access, identify and recruit a higher-level sponsor.

items.action: Probe competitive landscape during discovery: what Del Monte uses today for contract management (iContract scope), savings tracking (iSave scope), supplier collaboration (iSupplier scope), whether they are replacing an incumbent, whether an RFP or shortlist exists, and who else they are evaluating. Document in Competitors__c and competitive-context fields.
items.trigger: No competitors identified, current stack unknown
items.expected_effect: Understand competitive context, tailor positioning and differentiation (especially Merlin AI vs incumbent/competitors), and anticipate objections or evaluation criteria skewed by competitor influence.

confidence_signals.summary: Deal is one day old, created and qualified 2026-07-02. Champion Marcela actively connecting with internal procurement committee per Next Step same day. Engagement state is COOLING per server pulse (rep-initiated outreach 2026-07-02, awaiting buyer reply). No subsequent buyer activity recorded. Normal for a brand-new deal, but follow-up with Marcela and committee is needed within 7-10 days to maintain momentum.

north_star_verdict.math: Created 2026-07-02, qualified same day. 392 days to close (2027-07-30). Time in Qualified stage: 1 day. Typical enterprise cycle is 12-15 months (365-455 days); this deal has 392 days remaining, consistent with expected pace. No forward slip observable (deal is one day old). Champion Marcela connecting internally per Next Step dated 2026-07-02. At this stage (Qualified), the next milestone is discovery depth and multi-threading; no deliverables are overdue. Pace is appropriate for early-stage enterprise motion.

best_practice_check.summary: Deal is one day old and in early Qualified stage. Champion Marcela identified and actively connecting internally (2026-07-02). However, deal is currently single-threaded (only Marcela named), no contact roles recorded, no Economic Buyer or Decision Maker mapped, no MEDDPICC elements documented beyond champion mention, and no Avoma calls discovered. These are normal gaps for a brand-new opportunity; the immediate priority is to secure a discovery call with Marcela and the procurement committee to multi-thread, map roles, and begin MEDDPICC qualification.
